summary: Applied ADR 0133 on nginx-lv3 so portal services are authentication-by-default, with Keycloak-backed edge protection for ops, docs, and changelog and verified anonymous access blocked for Grafana.

verification.observed: A live HEAD request to https://ops.lv3.org/ on 2026-03-24 returned HTTP 302 with location https://ops.lv3.org/oauth2/sign_in?rd=https://ops.lv3.org/.
A live HEAD request to https://docs.lv3.org/ on 2026-03-24 returned HTTP 302 with location https://docs.lv3.org/oauth2/sign_in?rd=https://docs.lv3.org/ and x-robots-tag: noindex.
A live HEAD request to https://changelog.lv3.org/ on 2026-03-24 returned HTTP 302 with location https://changelog.lv3.org/oauth2/sign_in?rd=https://changelog.lv3.org/.
A live HEAD request to https://grafana.lv3.org/ on 2026-03-24 returned HTTP 302 with location /login instead of serving a dashboard anonymously.

verification.check: Protected portal config rendered on nginx-lv3
verification.observed: The targeted configure-edge-publication replay completed successfully after the protected-hostname lookup fix, and the live edge config rendered auth_request and /oauth2 locations for the protected portals.

notes: The first public-edge replay exposed a template lookup bug that left docs.lv3.org and changelog.lv3.org publicly reachable even though the policy data was correct. The live apply was re-run after fixing the hostname lookup in the NGINX template.
The shared edge auth cookie is now scoped to .lv3.org so a single Keycloak-backed browser session covers ops.lv3.org, docs.lv3.org, and changelog.lv3.org.
